problem.title: Heap Sort
problem.description: Implement heap sort algorithm to sort an array of integers in ascending order. Heap sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure.

example.explanation: Build max-heap, then repeatedly extract maximum to end of array. O(n log n), in-place.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Heap Sort Implementation
solutions.description: First build a max-heap from the array in O(n) by sifting down from the last non-leaf node to the root. Then repeatedly swap the root (the current maximum) with the last unsorted element and sift down the new root into the reduced heap to restore the heap property. The key insight is that each extraction places the largest remaining element in its final sorted position, achieving an in-place O(n log n) sort with no extra memory.
